name: Full quote-to-cash funnel across all pages
description: Capstone scenario: one customer journey traversing /assistant → /quotes → public-quote → /contracts → public-contract → /invoices → public-invoice → /dashboard. Every state transition must be visible to both the contractor and the customer at the right moment.

persona.name: Diego Martínez (contractor) + Asha Patel (customer)
persona.context: From 'I need a fence quote' to 'paid in full' in one continuous flow.

preconditions: Diego authenticated; Asha is a known client with email + phone
Stripe configured; SMS and email senders verified

steps.action: (Diego) on /assistant: 'New quote for Patel — fence repair, $350 labor, $80 materials'

steps.expect: Quote locks; email queued to Asha; appears in /quotes 'sent' stage

steps.action: (Asha) opens email, taps CTA, accepts on public quote page
steps.expect: Quote → decided-won; Diego sees activity event; assistant offers contract
request_terms_transition; phase = terms; assistant prompts deposit %, schedule, exclusions
Contract email queued; appears in /contracts as 'sent'
Contract → signed; Diego notified; deposit invoice auto-created
Asha receives within 1 minute; appears in /invoices unpaid

steps.action: (Diego) does the work; marks job complete

steps.expect: Job → fully paid; /dashboard outstanding decreases; /clients moves Patel out of 'active' to 'recent'

emails.subject: Your contract from Diego — fence repair

evaluationCriteria.check: Customer-facing pages share a coherent visual identity across quote → contract → invoice
Each transition feels deliberate — no orphaned states or 'now what?' moments for either actor
All state transitions are consistent: contractor and customer never see contradictory truth at the same time
Every customer-facing public page passes axe-core baseline; every contractor page has heading hierarchy that reads coherently
